variables.name: Medical History:Muscle disease
variables.description: The presence or absence of muscle disease in the subjects themselves as determined by clinician's interview; if present, the clinical interview will describe the type of muscle disease and thus may capture disorders that result in weakness, but are not considered to be a disease of the muscle.

variables.description: The presence or absence of obsessive compulsive disorder in the subjects themselves as determined by clinician's interview.

variables.description: The clinician interviewing the subject asks about any other medical illnesses and notes them as comments if they exist.

variables.description: The presence or absence of memory loss in the subjects themselves as determined by clinician's interview.

variables.name: Optional data:Handedness
variables.description: The subject has a hand preference (left, right), or uses both hands equally well (ambidextrous).

variables.name: Optional data:MMSE (enter score and date)
variables.description: The score obtained in the Folstein Mini Mental Status examination.

variables.name: Optional data:Neurological examination
variables.description: The subject either did (yes) or did not (no) undergo a neurological examination.

variables.name: Optional data:smoking history
variables.description: Record of an individual's background with regard to smoking tobacco. This would include such factors as start date, end date (if applicable), number of cigarettes smoked, attempts to quit, and others.

variables.name: Optional data:years smoking
variables.description: How many years did the person smoke ( if the person is a current or former smoker)?

variables.description: Subject's race

variables.description: Population/convenience control, spousal control, other control.

variables.name: Age
variables.description: Subject's age at sampling

variables.name: Age_At_Diagnosis
variables.description: Subject's age at time of diagnosis in years, or the month and year the subject was born.

variables.name: Atypical Features of ALS/MND:Atypical features of ALS/MND
variables.description: The clinician indicates, in the checkbox, if he/she observes other atypical features (i.e. sensory, autonomic, cerebellar, cognitive, Parkinsonian, sphincter, ocular, other) not normally associated with ALS.

variables.name: Catalog_ID
variables.description: Subject ID number assigned by NINDS Repository

variables.name: Clinical_Data_Source
variables.description: The source of the clinical data

variables.description: Type of clinician who collected the clinical data

variables.name: Country_Origin
variables.description: Subject's country of birth

variables.name: EMG Studies:Bulbar
variables.description: The clinician indicates if 'acute' or 'chronic' denervation, or 'acute' and 'chronic' denervation, or 'no denervation' is present in the bulbar regions of the body.  If the region was not examined, the clinician indicates 'not tested'.

variables.name: EMG Studies:Cervical/upper limbs
variables.description: The clinician indicates if 'acute' or 'chronic' denervation, or 'acute' and 'chronic' denervation, or 'no denervation' is present in the cervical region of the body.  If the region was not examined, the clinician indicates 'not tested'.

variables.name: EMG Studies:Lumbosacral/lower limbs
variables.description: The clinician indicates if 'acute' or 'chronic' denervation, or 'acute' and 'chronic' denervation, or 'no denervation' is present in the lumbo-sacral region of the body.  If the region was not examined, the clinician indicates 'not tested'.

variables.name: EMG Studies:Thoracic/chest
variables.description: The clinician indicates if 'acute' or 'chronic' denervation, or 'acute' and 'chronic' denervation, or 'no denervation' is present in the thoracic region of the body.  If the region was not examined, the clinician indicates 'not tested'.

variables.name: Ethnicity
variables.description: Ethnic category (as reported by subject) - check one:  Hispanic or Latino, Not Hispanic or Latino

variables.name: Family History:ALS/other MND
variables.description: 'Present' means that the individual subject indicates during clinician's interview that he/she has a blood relative who is known to have a history or diagnosis of ALS.  'Absent' means that he/she does not have a known blood relative with this disorder.  If present, the clinician is to determine which blood relatives have this disorder and their relationship to the proband.

variables.name: Family History:Alzheimer's disease
variables.description: 'Present'  means that the individual subject indicates during clinician's interview that he/she has a blood relative who is known to have a history or diagnosis of Alzheimer's disease.  'Absent' means that he/she does not have a known blood relative with this disorder.  If present, the clinician is to determine which blood relatives have this disorder and their relationship to the proband.

variables.name: Family History:Other dementia
variables.description: 'Present'  means that the individual subject indicates during clinician's interview that he/she has a blood relative who is known to have a history or diagnosis of dementia.  'Absent' means that he/she does not have a known blood relative with this disorder.  If present, the clinician is to determine which blood relatives have this disorder and their relationship to the proband.

variables.name: Family History:Other neurodegenerative disease
variables.description: 'Present' means that the individual subject indicates during clinician's interview that he/she has a blood relative who is known to have a history or diagnosis of another neurodegenerative disease.  'Absent' means that he/she does not have a known blood relative with any other neurodegenerative disease.  If present, the clinician is to determine which blood relatives have a neurodegenerative disease and their relationship to the proband.

variables.name: Family History:Parkinson's disease
variables.description: 'Present' means that the individual subject indicates during clinician's interview that he/she has a blood relative who is known to have a history or diagnosis of Parkinson's disease.  'Absent' means that he/she does not have a known blood relative with this disorder.  If present, the clinician is to determine which blood relatives have this disorder and their relationship to the proband.

variables.name: Gender
variables.description: Subject's gender

variables.name: Genetics:Other mutation
variables.description: The clinician indicates if testing for any genetic mutation predisposing the subject to ALS was done; if so, were any gene mutations present or not?

variables.name: Genetics:SOD-1 mutation
variables.description: The clinician indicates if the subject underwent genetic testing for a SOD-1 mutation.  If tests were completed, was a gene mutation present or not?

variables.name: In_Panel
variables.description: Indicates whether DNA of this subject is included in any panels available through the NINDS Repository catalog.

variables.name: Longitudinal Data: Is this data Longitudinal (Follow-Up) Data?
variables.description: Is the data longitudinal or an initial assessment?

variables.name: Medical History:Alzheimer's disease
variables.description: 'Present' means that the individual subject indicates during clinician's interview that there is a medical history of Alzheimer's disease.  'Absent' means that the subject does not have a medical history of the disease.

variables.name: Medical History:Ataxia
variables.description: 'Present' means that the individual subject indicates during clinician's interview that there is a medical history of ataxia.  'Absent' means that the subject does not have a medical history of the disease.

variables.name: Medical History:Autism
variables.description: 'Present' means that the individual subject indicates during clinician's interview that there is a medical history of autism.  'Absent' means that the subject does not have a medical history of the disease.

variables.name: Medical History:Bipolar (manic-depressive)
variables.description: 'Present' means that the individual subject indicates during clinician's interview that there is a medical history of bipolar disorder.  'Absent' means that the subject does not have a medical history of the disorder.

variables.name: Medical History:Brain aneurysm
variables.description: 'Present' means that the individual subject indicates during clinician's interview that there is a medical history of brain aneurysm.  'Absent' means that the subject does not have a medical history of the disorder.

variables.name: Medical History:Cancer
variables.description: 'Present' means that the individual subject indicates during clinician's interview that there is a medical history of cancer.  'Absent' means that the subject does not have a medical history of the disorder.

variables.name: Medical History:Dementia
variables.description: 'Present' means that the individual subject indicates during clinician's interview that there is a medical history of dementia.  'Absent' means that the subject does not have a medical history of the disorder.

variables.name: Medical History:Depression
variables.description: 'Present' means that the individual subject indicates during clinician's interview that there is a medical history of depression.  'Absent' means that the subject does not have a medical history of the disorder.

variables.name: Medical History:Diabetes
variables.description: 'Present' means that the individual subject indicates during clinician's interview that there is a medical history of diabetes.  'Absent' means that the subject does not have a medical history of the disorder.

variables.name: Medical History:Dystonia
variables.description: 'Present' means that the individual subject indicates during clinician's interview that there is a medical history of dystonia.  'Absent' means that the subject does not have a medical history of the disorder.

variables.name: Medical History:Epilepsy
variables.description: 'Present' means that the individual subject indicates during clinician's interview that there is a medical history of epilepsy.  'Absent' means that the subject does not have a medical history of the disorder.

variables.name: Medical History:Heart disease
variables.description: 'Present' means that the individual subject indicates during clinician's interview that there is a medical history of heart disease.  'Absent' means that the subject does not have a medical history of the disorder.

variables.name: Medical History:Hypertension
variables.description: 'Present' means that the individual subject indicates during clinician's interview that there is a medical history of hypertension.  'Absent' means that the subject does not have a medical history of the disorder.

variables.name: Medical History:Multiple sclerosis
variables.description: 'Present' means that the individual subject indicates during clinician's interview that there is a medical history of multiple sclerosis.  'Absent' means that the subject does not have a medical history of the disorder.

variables.name: Medical History:Muscle disease
variables.description: 'Present' means that the individual subject indicates during clinician's interview that there is a medical history of muscle disease.  'Absent' means that the subject does not have a medical history of the disorder.

variables.name: Medical History:Parkinson's
variables.description: 'Present' means that the individual subject indicates during clinician's interview that there is a medical history of Parkinson's disease.  'Absent' means that the subject does not have a medical history of the disorder.

variables.name: Medical History:Schizophrenia
variables.description: 'Present' means that the individual subject indicates during clinician's interview that there is a medical history of schizophrenia.  'Absent' means that the subject does not have a medical history of the disorder.

variables.name: Medical History:Stroke
variables.description: 'Present' means that the individual subject indicates during clinician's interview that there is a medical history of  stroke.  'Absent' means that the subject does not have a medical history of the disorder.

variables.name: Medical History:Suicide/Attempt
variables.description: 'Present' means that the individual subject indicates during clinician's interview that there is a medical history of attempted suicide.  'Absent' means that the subject does not have a medical history of attempted suicide.

variables.name: Onset_Age
variables.description: The age at which the subjects themselves first noted symptoms of ALS, based on clinician's interview.

variables.name: Optional data:Escorial criteria
variables.description: Escorial criteria: definite, probable, lab-supported probable, possible, suspected; reference: Brooks BR, Miller RG, Swash M, Munsat TL.  El Escorial revisited: revised criteria for the diagnosis of amyotrophic lateral sclerosis.  Amyotroph. Lateral Scler. Other Motor Neuron Disord. 2000; 1:293-299.

variables.name: Optional data:Handedness
variables.description: The hand preference of the subject; the subject's dominant hand.

variables.name: Optional data:smoking history
variables.description: A record of an individual's background with regard to smoking tobacco. This would include such factors as start date, end date (if applicable), number of cigarettes smoked, attempts to quit, and others.

variables.name: Primary Clinical Diagnosis:Primary clinical diagnosis
variables.description: The clinician indicates which type of MND the subject has by choosing one of the offered diagnoses (ALS, progressive muscular atrophy, primary lateral sclerosis, other), or by writing in the type of MND if the best diagnosis is not offered.

variables.name: Race
variables.description: Subject's race

variables.name: Secondary Neurological Diagnosis:Secondary neurological diagnoses
variables.description: The clinician indicates whether the patient has fronto-temporal dementia (or any other neurological impairment, or NA).

variables.name: Signs Supporting Diagnosis:Lower Motor Neuron Signs-Bulbar
variables.description: The clinician indicates if and where there are 'definite', 'indeterminate', or 'absent' lower motor neuron signs in the bulbar region.  If the region was not examined, the clinician indicates 'not tested'.

variables.name: Signs Supporting Diagnosis:Lower Motor Neuron Signs-Cervical/upper limbs
variables.description: The clinician indicates if and where there are 'definite', 'indeterminate', or 'absent' lower motor neuron signs in the cervical region.  If the region was not examined, the clinician indicates 'not tested'.

variables.name: Signs Supporting Diagnosis:Lower Motor Neuron Signs-Lumbosacral/lower limbs
variables.description: The clinician indicates if and where there are 'definite', 'indeterminate', or 'absent' lower motor neuron signs in the lumbo-sacral region.  If the region was not examined, the clinician indicates 'not tested'.

variables.name: Signs Supporting Diagnosis:Lower Motor Neuron Signs-Thoracic/chest
variables.description: The clinician indicates if and where there are 'definite', 'indeterminate', or 'absent' lower motor neuron signs in the thoracic region.  If the region was not examined, the clinician indicates 'not tested'.

variables.name: Signs Supporting Diagnosis:Upper Motor Neuron Signs-Bulbar
variables.description: The clinician indicates if and where there are 'definite', 'indeterminate', or 'absent' upper motor neuron signs in the bulbar region.  If the region was not examined, the clinician indicates 'not tested'.

variables.name: Signs Supporting Diagnosis:Upper Motor Neuron Signs-Cervical/upper limbs
variables.description: The clinician indicates if and where there are 'definite', 'indeterminate', or 'absent' upper motor neuron signs in the cervical region.  If the region was not examined, the clinician indicates 'not tested'.

variables.name: Signs Supporting Diagnosis:Upper Motor Neuron Signs-Lumbosacral/lower limbs
variables.description: The clinician indicates if and where there are 'definite', 'indeterminate', or 'absent' upper motor neuron signs in the lumbo-sacral region.  If the region was not examined, the clinician indicates 'not tested'.

variables.name: Signs Supporting Diagnosis:Upper Motor Neuron Signs-Thoracic/chest
variables.description: The clinician indicates if and where there are 'definite', 'indeterminate', or 'absent' upper motor neuron signs in the thoracic region.  If the region was not examined, the clinician indicates 'not tested'.

variables.name: Site of Symptom Onset:site of symptom onset
variables.description: The clinician indicates the site of onset of the progressive weakness (i.e. bulbar, truncal, generalized, respiratory, limb).

variables.name: Treatment:Current treatment
variables.description: The clinician indicates the treatment that is currently applied (Riluzole, percutaneous endoscopic gastrostomy, non-invasive positive pressure ventilation, tracheotomy, other).

variables.name: Zip_Abbrev
variables.description: First 3 digits of subject's zip code

tables.description: This data table contains diagnostic information of Italian and US subjects with diagnosis of ALS (ALS, site of onset) or control, and provides general sociodemographic information (birthplace, gender, ethnicity).  For details, please see: Hum Mol Genet. 2009 Apr 15;18(8):1524-32; PMID 19193627).
